steps.title: Find the cost after the first two hours
steps.content: The first $2$ hours cost $160$. A $6$-hour repair costs $360$, so the additional $4$ hours cost $$360-160=200.$$

steps.title: Convert that to an hourly fee
steps.content: The additional $4$ hours cost $200$, so the hourly fee is $$\frac{200}{4}=50.$$

steps.title: Build the function
steps.content: For $x\geq 2$, start with the $160$ charge for the first $2$ hours, then add $50$ for each hour beyond $2$: $$f(x)=160+50(x-2)=50x+60.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The correct model is $f(x)=50x+60$. It gives $f(2)=160$ and $f(6)=360$, matching both facts, so A is correct.
